inline_comments.message: such change will require change in the SDNC request so in the vf-module config assign BB. We have "vf-module-model-customization-uuid - why we cannot take both values: "vf-module-model-invariant-id and vf-module-model-version-id base on customization id. I think we can. Moreover, in order to have k8s-rb-definiton-name and its version base on this parameters first we need to change artifact broker in multicloud.
the name of profile can be common for all vf-modules. in this case, in VNF template we can define is an input and on vf-module level templates it should be taken from mdsal

This was Eric's proposal to add these. If we can get values somehow from xxx-customization-uuid then fine, let's talk about how to do that.

I'm not sure why we would need to wait SDNC changes or multicloud broker changes before adding these?

I understood that we have profile for each vf-module = kube app? 

And in general don't like the mdsal as storing place (the plan is to get rid of it) as cds can store resolutions itself fine.

please change the name to be k8s specific template name such as vFW_CDS_K8S. 

NOTE: We already have a vFW_CDS for openstack use case which is using the template name of vFW_CDS.
Yes, we are still considering if it's possible to use same model for both or create completely own model for k8s. I think it would be better the exact same model would for for both.
vf-module-model-invariant-uuid instead

vf-module-model-version instead 

why we do not use the same format like for other data types? I understand that this is not required?
"${vf-module-model-invariant-uuid}"
"${vf-module-model-version}"

You are right, we do not have to wait for changes in the broker. The changes in sdnc request are not need what I explain in different comment.

We will have separate profiles but we can give them the same name. So this name can be given as input on vnf level template and on vf-module level it will be read from db. If we can store it in cds db instead of SDNC DB, why not - value on vnf level should be stored only for vf-module-level
ok

ok.

What format? If you mean -/_ I had the same question in the review of our baseline. As "traditionally" cds models have been using format xx-yy and for some reason the latest updates in vFW model has added many variables in the format xx_yy. So what format we should use. I would prefer the xx-yy for cds models.
yes if we do it that way.
ok
ok

Template-Name: vFW_CDS
Template-Version: 1.0.0

Both must be added to TOSCA-meta
